cards.front: What is the purpose of the '*' wild card in the ls command?
cards.back: To display files and directories in a directory and its subdirectories

cards.front: How does the '?' wild card work in the ls command?
cards.back: It matches any single character in a file name

cards.front: What does the '**' wild card represent in the ls command?
cards.back: A glob pattern for all files and directories in the current directory and its subdirectories

cards.front: What is the difference between '*' and '?' wild cards in the ls command?
cards.back: *. matches any characters including spaces, while ? matches only one character

cards.front: What is the purpose of the '{}' wild card in the ls command?
cards.back: It is an extended glob pattern and can be used with more complex file name matching
